name: WhatToCharge Pricing Rates Dataset
description: Typical 2026 US price ranges for 195+ service, trade, and maker professions, in each trade's natural unit.

price_factors: Home size and number of bathrooms (bathrooms and kitchens take the longest)
Standard clean vs. deep clean vs. move-out clean
How often the client books (weekly clients should get a lower rate than one-offs)
Your local cost of living and how booked your calendar is
Surface type, concrete, wood, vinyl, and brick clean at different speeds
How dirty it is (heavy mildew or oil stains take longer and need treatment)
Height and access (second-story siding is slower and riskier)
Water access and whether you supply it
Lot size and how much is actual mowable grass
Obstacles, trees, beds, and tight gates slow you down
Whether trimming, edging, and blowing are included
Number of dogs from the same household
Peak demand windows (midday weekdays book out first)
In your home vs. overnight at the client's house
Number of pets and any medication or special needs
Holidays (Thanksgiving and December book out and justify a premium)
Daytime drop-in visits vs. true overnight stays
Extra duties, cooking, bath time, light tidying
Number of initial concepts and revision rounds
Deliverables, just the logo vs. full brand kit, files, and usage rights
Client size (a local bakery and a funded startup do not pay the same)
Topic complexity and required research or expertise
Content type, blog post vs. whitepaper vs. sales copy
Whether SEO, interviews, or editing rounds are included
Stack and complexity (a brochure site vs. a custom web app)
Design included vs. building to a provided design
Posts per week and whether you create the content or just schedule it
Paid ad management (usually a separate line item)
Vehicle size (sedan vs. SUV vs. truck)
Condition, pet hair, stains, and heavy grime add real time
Add-ons like ceramic coating or engine bay cleaning
Hours of work (be honest, count them)
Wall condition and prep (patching, sanding, priming)
Ceiling height and number of coats
Paint quality and who supplies it
Linear feet of gutter and home size
Debris level and time since last cleaning
Task skill level and tools required
Volume of raw footage to sort
Complexity: motion graphics, color grade, sound design
Skill level and specialized tasks (design, ads, dev)
Stain severity and pet odor treatment
Stairs and hallways (priced separately by most pros)
Carpet type and how long since the last clean
Truck volume the load fills (1/4, 1/2, full)
Dump and landfill fees in your area
Heavy or special items (appliances, mattresses, tires)
Stairs, distance to the truck, and labor
Pool size and type (chlorine vs. salt)
Filament or resin used (grams) and material type
Print time (machine hours tie up your printer)
Design or modeling work vs. printing a provided file
Number of servings (base price per slice)
Design complexity: tiers, sculpting, sugar flowers
Delivery and setup for tiered cakes
Wax type, fragrance load, and vessel quality
Labor and batch size (per-unit time drops at scale)
Branding and whether it is retail or wholesale
Shop hours including milling, joinery, and finishing
Service type: cut, color, highlights, treatment
Service: manicure, gel, acrylic, dip, extensions
Removal and repair of previous work
Number of concepts and revision rounds
Usage and licensing (print run, ad spend)
Scope: technical, content, link building, local
Site size and competitiveness of the niche
One-time audit / setup vs. ongoing retainer
Usage: internal, web, broadcast, national ad
Equipment: sound, lighting, extra speakers, MC duties
Service style: drop-off, buffet, plated, full-service
Your niche and track record (executive coaching pays most)
Private session vs. studio group class
Materials and panel or wiring complexity
License and emergency vs. scheduled call
Travel and whether parts must be ordered
Service type: hardware, software, data recovery
Home size, stories, and surface area
Paint quality and number of coats
Scope: cleanup, planting, full redesign, hardscaping
Proximity to structures and power lines
Hole size and number of patches
Removal and disposal of old flooring
Home size and severity of infestation
Terrain and slope (sloped yards are slower)
Gates and post setting in concrete
Whether you supply the lights or use theirs
Vehicle make (European and luxury run higher)
Pets, plants, or a pool to maintain
Copywriting and content provided or not
Copy type (web, email, ads, sales page)
Turnaround and verbatim vs. clean copy
Copy and design included or not
Batch size (per-unit cost drops at scale)
Simple hem vs. full resize or reconstruction
Number of colors and print locations
Beard trim and hot-towel shave add-ons
Your experience and the event's stakes
Guest count and number of bartenders
Whether you supply alcohol and mixers
Roof size, pitch, and number of facets
Tear-off of the old roof and layers
Home size and number of stories
Removal and disposal of old siding
Trim, corners, and house wrap detail
Tile type and size (large format and mosaic are slower)
Number of cabinets and linear feet
Leveling on uneven walls and floors
Wash only vs. wash and wax
Whether you supply water and power
Vehicle size and number of windows
Full car vs. front two windows
Design and print for commercial graphics
Per room vs. hourly vs. percentage of budget
Number of organizers on the job
Home size and number of vents
Wall prep and old paper removal
Letter of inquiry vs. full proposal
Editing level (cuts vs. full production with music and mixing)
Retouch level (basic color vs. high-end skin, composite)
Modeling from scratch vs. provided files
Content and storytelling vs. polish only
Flat fee vs. percentage of spend
Tank size and type (freshwater, saltwater, reef)
Gym contract vs. your own class
Full vs. partial vs. day-of coordination
Percentage of budget vs. flat fee
Level and goal (hobby vs. audition, pro)
Level (kids intro vs. adult career switch)
Size and complexity (plant hanger vs. large wall art)
